statusLine: EU fuel security remains under strain with 17 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel type despite overall stock levels above mandatory minimums.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure as oil prices surge to $101.46 per barrel, marking a 40% increase since late February's Middle East supply disruptions. While EU-wide averages remain above the 90-day mandatory minimum with petrol at 110.1 days and diesel at 100.5 days, the distribution across member states reveals concerning vulnerabilities.

Romania presents the most severe situation with critical reserves across all fuel types: just 33.2 days of petrol, 32.4 days of diesel, and 76.4 days of jet fuel. Poland and Luxembourg also face critical shortages in all three categories. Beyond these acute cases, 14 additional countries maintain critical reserves in at least one fuel type, indicating widespread strain on national stockpiles following recent strategic reserve releases.

Jet fuel emerges as the most constrained category at 94.9 days EU-wide, with 18 of 27 countries below safe levels. This aviation fuel shortage coincides with the broader supply disruptions affecting European energy markets. Price pressures are evident across the bloc, with petrol averaging €1.81 per liter and reaching €2.39 in the Netherlands, reflecting the underlying supply tensions despite adequate aggregate stock levels.
keyPoints: 17 of 27 EU countries hold critical reserves in at least one fuel type, with Romania, Poland, and Luxembourg critical across all three categories
Jet fuel shows the tightest supply at 94.9 days EU-wide, with 18 of 27 countries below safe levels
Oil prices have surged 40% since late February to $101.46 per barrel following Middle East supply disruptions
Wide price variations across the EU reflect supply strain, with petrol ranging from €1.34 in Malta to €2.39 in the Netherlands
Despite critical individual country situations, EU aggregate reserves remain above the 90-day mandatory minimum for all fuel types
